text.title: Funnel Optimization 7-Phase Process
text.summary: The 7-phase process for systematically improving conversion: map, measure, find drop, diagnose, hypothesize, test, repeat.

rule.statement: Always fix the step with the largest absolute user loss first, not the step with the lowest percentage conversion.
rule.rationale: A step losing 6,500 users at 35% conversion outweighs a step losing 300 users at 9% conversion, even though the percentage looks worse on the smaller step.

rule.statement: Define every step from first touch to goal, name each step with an event name, and get stakeholder agreement before measuring.
rule.rationale: Ambiguous step definitions cause tracking inconsistencies that make drop-off analysis unreliable.

example.note: SaaS signup funnel with concrete event names at each step: page_view_home, cta_clicked, signup_form_viewed, email_submitted, email_verified, profile_completed, first_action_completed.

rule.statement: Collect minimum 30 days of data and segment by traffic source, device, and new vs. returning before drawing conclusions.
rule.rationale: Short windows miss weekly patterns; unsegmented data hides that mobile users drop at different steps than desktop users.

rule.statement: Rank transitions by absolute users lost, not by drop percentage. Prioritize the transition with the highest user count lost.
rule.rationale: A 65% drop at step 1→2 losing 6,500 users is higher priority than a 30% drop at step 6→7 losing 461 users.

section.title: Phase 4: Diagnose the Cause
section.p: Use multiple diagnostic methods: session recordings reveal user behavior; heatmaps show attention areas; exit surveys capture stated reasons; user interviews provide depth.

list.item: Landing to CTA: unclear value prop, poor design, slow load
CTA to form: too many fields, scary commitment language
Form to submit: technical errors, confusing UX
Submit to verify: email in spam, long delay
Onboarding to activation: too complex, no guidance, value not shown

rule.statement: Write every hypothesis in IF/THEN/BECAUSE format: "If we [change X], then [metric] will improve by [Y%], because [diagnosis-based reason]."
rule.rationale: Vague hypotheses cannot be falsified; format enforces that each hypothesis is grounded in diagnosis, not guesswork.

rule.statement: Prioritize hypotheses with ICE (Impact x Confidence x Ease). Test the highest ICE score first. Score 20-30 means test immediately.
rule.rationale: Without scoring, teams test what is easiest to build, not what will move the metric most.

rule.statement: After implementing a winner, move to the next biggest drop-off transition. Track cumulative conversion improvement over time.
rule.rationale: Funnel optimization is a continuous loop, not a one-time project.

list.item: Marketing: Impression → Click → Landing Page → Signup → Activation → Purchase
SaaS: Visit → Signup → Onboarding → Activation → Engagement → Conversion → Retention
E-commerce: Visit → View Product → Add to Cart → Checkout Start → Payment → Confirmation
Mobile App: App Store → Download → Open → Onboarding → Core Action → Retention → Monetization
